title: ononis natrix subsp. inaequalifolia
intro: Ononis natrix subsp. inaequalifolia, commonly known as Hairy Jointed Burnet, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Hairy Jointed Burnet include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various diseases such as arthritis and certain types of cancer. The plant's therapeutic actions include expectorant, anti-diarrheal, and anti-spasmodic properties, and it contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins.
side_effects: Ononis natrix subsp. inaequalifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Excessive consumption may also lead to cardiovascular issues, including palpitations and hypotension. Furthermore, individuals with pre-existing kidney or liver conditions should avoid using the plant altogether to prevent exacerbating their conditions.
